note: Every claim carries all five invariants. scripts/check-admissibility-registry.mjs resolves each verifier_behavior and negative_vector against the tree and fails the build on any gap. Claims that cannot yet satisfy all five (e.g. gate-layer reporting artifacts pending a negative conformance vector) are NOT listed until they can.

claims.claim: A named human with a device-bound passkey approved this exact action under user verification (biometric or PIN).
claims.pinned_authority: The approver's credential public key, pinned from server-side enrollment (approver_credentials). Never a key carried in the receipt, and the pinned key's class governs the verification routine.

negative_vector.case: pinned Class-A key presented with a key_class:B bare signature (downgrade) is refused

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the WebAuthn assertion and signature chain check out. ACCEPTED = the verifying key was pinned from enrollment and its pinned class was enforced, so a receipt-supplied or downgraded key can never elevate to Class-A.

claims.claim: M distinct named humans, none of them the initiator, each bound their own device assertion to the same exact action within a time window.
claims.pinned_authority: The quorum policy from an org-pinned template keyed by (org, action_type) (lib/guard-quorum-template.js), and each approver_public_key from enrollment. Both sourced out of band, never from the quorum document.

negative_vector.case: initiator counted as an approver, and one device key filling two seats when distinct_humans is disabled, are both refused

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the members are internally consistent with the policy and keys handed to the verifier (threshold, distinct humans, distinct keys, initiator excluded, roster, window, order). ACCEPTED = that policy is the org's real one from the pinned template and the keys came from enrollment. Internal consistency against creator-declared inputs is not authorization.

claims.claim: The authority, delegation, or key backing this action is still current within a stated freshness bound.
claims.pinned_authority: The revocation authority's signing key, pinned by the relying party. The staleness bound itself is a relying-party policy input, graded per consequence.

negative_vector.case: a revoked target and a stale/expired revocation statement fail safe rather than open

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the revocation statement is authentic under the pinned authority key. ACCEPTED = the relying party's own staleness bound is met; stale material fails closed, never open.

claims.claim: This receipt existed no later than a specific time, proven by an RFC 3161 token from a trusted timestamp authority.
claims.pinned_authority: The TSA's public key, pinned by the relying party. An unpinned or unknown TSA is refused.

negative_vector.case: a token from an unpinned TSA and a messageImprint that does not match the receipt digest are refused

claims.acceptance_semantics: VERIFIED = the RFC 3161 token is well-formed and its messageImprint matches the receipt digest. ACCEPTED = the token was issued by a TSA the relying party pinned; time from an untrusted TSA carries no weight.
